My Answer: B (6th week)
Your analysis is correct based on current understanding of human embryology. During early fetal development, specific stages mark key milestones for organ systems including teeth. Initial steps towards dentition indeed begin approximately during the sixth to seventh weeks post-fertilization when the dental lamina forms along the midline from where future tooth buds will develop.

**Answer:** B (6th week)
